description: NOTE: the `tier` field in these vectors is STIPULATED to isolate the op-laws under test; tier DERIVATION from data is the tier-vectors.json family (backlog #19, layered anchor). Tailoring resolution laws (D13 / spec Appendix B): ordered application; two operations addressing the same target within one Tailoring = validation error (chaining is the only override); weakening ops need an attached Deviation at CONSUMER tier; Authority-tier Tailorings are normative source and owe none (D13 rev, backlog #2); excludes are selection, never weakening. Target key = requirement-ref + statement-id + (parameter | field | facet where applicable).

vectors.name: same-target-conflict
vectors.reason: two operations share one target within one Tailoring - overrides happen auditable via Tailoring-of-Tailoring chaining, never silently

vectors.name: same-statement-different-parameters-no-conflict
vectors.reason: the target key includes the parameter name

vectors.name: consumer-easing-without-deviation
vectors.reason: easing without a record is how baselines rot invisibly (B.1.6 failure text)

vectors.name: consumer-easing-with-deviation

deviation.rationale: Compensating control X accepted by CISO.

vectors.reason: the audited escape working as designed

vectors.name: authority-easing-no-deviation-required
vectors.reason: an Authority-tier Tailoring is normative source - publishing variance is not deviating from oneself (D13 rev, backlog #2); the easing still classifies and reports

vectors.name: exclude-is-selection-never-deviation

excludes.rationale: out of scope for classification P

vectors.reason: baselines are made of exclusions (ISM x5; CR26 classes); a Deviation per exclusion would drown them (D13, rejected P7-B2)

vectors.name: tightening-op-monotone
vectors.reason: upward along a drawn lattice line is free - the everyday hardening of every elevated baseline

vectors.name: substantive-prose-without-deviation
vectors.reason: substantive prose replacement requires a Deviation; whether new prose is softer is undecidable, so the author's declaration is what gets audited

vectors.name: consumer-set-parameter-over-tightening-without-deviation
vectors.reason: loosening a tightening:lower deadline 6h->24h is a weakening; without a Deviation at consumer tier it must fail (D13 set-parameter law; the P6-F2 evasion backdoor - backlog #25)

vectors.name: consumer-set-parameter-over-tightening-with-deviation

deviation.rationale: Extended reporting window accepted for low-severity class.

vectors.reason: the audited escape channel makes the loosening legal and on the record

vectors.name: authority-set-parameter-over-tightening-no-deviation-required
vectors.reason: an Authority-tier Tailoring is normative source (D13 rev, backlog #2); the loosening still classifies and reports

vectors.name: consumer-set-parameter-out-of-choices
vectors.reason: binding a choice parameter to a value outside its declared set needs a Deviation (P6-F2: the md5-outside-choices demo) - without one at consumer tier it fails

vectors.name: consumer-remove-required-relation-without-deviation
vectors.reason: removing a `required` dependency edge is a weakening of the graph (B.3); needs a Deviation at consumer tier (backlog #25)

vectors.name: consumer-remove-required-relation-with-deviation

deviation.rationale: Dependency satisfied by an equivalent compensating control.

vectors.reason: the audited escape channel working as designed

vectors.name: consumer-detach-of-pinned-selection-facet-without-deviation
vectors.reason: P10 #29: the pin declares [selection]; detaching it eases scoping - Deviation duty at consumer tier (D13 table)

vectors.name: consumer-detach-of-pinned-selection-facet-with-deviation

rationale.en: Scoping facet superseded by contract-level scoping.

vectors.reason: P10 #29: same detach through the audited escape channel

vectors.name: attach-of-unregistered-facet-is-an-error-at-any-tier
vectors.reason: P10 #29: neither stdlib, nor pinned, nor private: - dangerous-by-default (D10), an error at ANY tier, not a Deviation duty

vectors.name: attach-of-private-facet-is-free
vectors.reason: private: carries modifies-semantics [] by definition (D10) - no duty, no registration
